formulas.name: The Law of Drainage
formulas.description: The law states that the path of a stream or river is the shortest route between two points, and it follows the slope of the land.
formulas.notes: Helps in understanding the pattern and direction of drainage.

formulas.name: The Concept of Watershed
formulas.description: A watershed is an area of land that separates two drainage systems and where water flows in different directions.
formulas.notes: Helps in understanding the concept of drainage divide.

formulas.name: The Concept of Drainage Basin
formulas.description: A drainage basin is an area of land that drains water into a particular water body such as a river, lake, or ocean.
formulas.notes: Helps in understanding the concept of catchment area.

formulas.name: The Law of Equipotential Surface
formulas.description: The equipotential surface is a surface on which the water table is at the same level, indicating the direction of groundwater flow.
formulas.notes: Helps in understanding the direction of groundwater flow.

formulas.name: The Concept of Water Cycle
formulas.description: The water cycle is the continuous process of water movement on, above, and below the surface of the Earth.
formulas.notes: Helps in understanding the movement of water on the Earth's surface.

formulas.name: The Concept of Runoff
formulas.description: Runoff is the water that flows over the land surface and into streams, rivers, and lakes.
formulas.notes: Helps in understanding the process of water flow.

formulas.name: The Concept of Infiltration
formulas.description: Infiltration is the process by which water seeps into the soil and becomes groundwater.
formulas.notes: Helps in understanding the process of water absorption.
